occurrence_sentence: # The Quantum Factory
When the word 'factory' pops up — it doesn't matter where it is — I think of something on a grand scale, producing thousands of items per hour.
But that's exactly what's happened in Germany with the quantum computing (QC) startup [Quantum Factory Gmbh](https://quantum-factory.de/).
Founded by businessman and entrepreneur Alexander Hüsers and quantum scientist Dr. Björn Stein in 2019, the startup has grand plans in the QC ecosystem.
Founded by businessman and entrepreneur Alexander Hüsers and quantum scientist Dr. Björn Stein in 2019, the startup has grand plans in the QC ecosystem.

Founded by businessman and entrepreneur [Alexander Hüsers](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alexander-h%C3%BCsers-a979a5151?originalSubdomain=de) and quantum scientist [Dr. Björn Stein](https://www.linkedin.com/in/bj%C3%B6rn-stein-5bb25135/) in 2019, the startup has grand plans in the QC ecosystem.

Founded by businessman and entrepreneur [Alexander Hüsers](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alexander-h%C3%BCsers-a979a5151?originalSubdomain=de) and quantum scientist [Dr. Björn Stein](https://www.linkedin.com/in/bj%C3%B6rn-stein-5bb25135/) in 2019, the startup has grand plans in the QC ecosystem.
And though it may be slow going, there is little doubt QC is heading in the right direction.
And Stein, who received his Ph.D. from Hannover University in contributions to a single Ytterbium ion-optical atomic clock, is well-qualified to guide the startup in its R&D endeavours.
And Stein, who received his Ph.D. from Hannover University in contributions to a single Ytterbium ion-optical atomic clock, is well-qualified to guide the startup in its R&D endeavours.
James Dargan is a writer and researcher at The Quantum Insider.
